name: Sima Chi (Fengdu)
page_title: Sima Chi (Fengdu) 司馬熾 (豐度) [Jin] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

sections.short-biography: Third emperor of Jin. Captured and killed by northern barbarians.
sections.details:
Traditional Chinese 
	司馬熾 (豐度)


	 Pronunciation 
	Si1ma3 Chi4 (Feng1du4)


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	SÄ«mÇ Chì (Fēngdù)


	 Wade-Giles 
	Ssŭ-ma Ch‘ih (Fêng-tu)
sections.biography-source-undefined:
“The Filial Cherished August Emperor, taboo name [Sima] Chi, styled Fengdu, was the twenty-fifth son of the Martial Emperor. In the first year of the Taixi era [290], he was enfeoffed as Commandery Prince of Yuzhang. During the reign of the Filial and Mild [Emperor], the imperial clan began to decline, yet the Emperor always maintained his moral character even as a child. His gates were closed to guests and travelers, and he did not interact with worldly affairs. He engrossed himself in historical records for leisure. He had such a reputation in this era. He was previously conferred to be 散騎常侍, but when the Prince of Zhao; [Sima] Lun usurped the throne, [Sima Chi] was arrested. When [Sima] Lun fell, [Sima Chi] became 射聲校尉. He gradually shifted to serve as Generalissimo of Chariots and Cavalry and Commander of Various Military Affairs in Qing Province. He never went to the defenses there. In the first year of the Yongxing era [304], he shifted to become Generalissimo who Garrisons the North and Commander of Various Military Affairs for the Defense of Ye City.”

孝懷皇帝諱熾，字豐度，武帝第二十五子也。太熙元年，封豫章郡王。屬孝惠之時，宗室構禍，帝沖素自守，門絕賓游，不交世事，專玩史籍，有譽於時。初拜散騎常侍，及趙王倫篡，見收。倫敗，為射聲校尉。累遷車騎大將軍、都督青州諸軍事。未之鎮。永興元年，改授鎮北大將軍、都督鄴城守諸軍事。

In 311, Luoyang was captured by the barbarian forces of Liu Yao. He was made Duke of Kuaiji but was killed two years later. His death was in March 15, 313 (Gregorian).

full_text:
Sima Chi (Fengdu) 司馬熾 (豐度)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Sima Chi (司馬熾), styledFengdu (豐度). Third emperor of Jin. Captured and killed by northern barbarians. Sima Chi was affiliated with the Jin Dynasty. Sima Chi lived during the Jin Dynasty periods of Chinese history.
Return to theThree Kingdoms Encyclopediaor browse theEncyclopedia Directory.

Short Biography
Third emperor of Jin. Captured and killed by northern barbarians.

“The Filial Cherished August Emperor, taboo name [Sima] Chi, styled Fengdu, was the twenty-fifth son of the Martial Emperor. In the first year of the Taixi era [290], he was enfeoffed as Commandery Prince of Yuzhang. During the reign of the Filial and Mild [Emperor], the imperial clan began to decline, yet the Emperor always maintained his moral character even as a child. His gates were closed to guests and travelers, and he did not interact with worldly affairs. He engrossed himself in historical records for leisure. He had such a reputation in this era. He was previously conferred to be 散騎常侍, but when the Prince of Zhao; [Sima] Lun usurped the throne, [Sima Chi] was arrested. When [Sima] Lun fell, [Sima Chi] became 射聲校尉. He gradually shifted to serve as Generalissimo of Chariots and Cavalry and Commander of Various Military Affairs in Qing Province. He never went to the defenses there. In the first year of the Yongxing era [304], he shifted to become Generalissimo who Garrisons the North and Commander of Various Military Affairs for the Defense of Ye City.”
